special_surfaces.name: event
special_surfaces.description: Observe all OpenCode events through one handler.
special_surfaces.guidance: Use this for session.idle or any workflow that coordinates multiple event types.

special_surfaces.name: tool
special_surfaces.description: Register custom tools from a plugin.
special_surfaces.guidance: Requires the official @opencode-ai/plugin helper in the documented examples.

events.name: command.executed
events.description: React after an OpenCode command executes.
events.guidance: Prefer lightweight bookkeeping, logging, or follow-up context.

events.name: file.edited
events.guidance: Use this when the plugin needs file-level bookkeeping beyond tool-name checks.

events.name: file.watcher.updated
events.guidance: Good for syncing state from watcher-driven changes.

events.name: installation.updated
events.guidance: Use this for setup reminders or dependency-related follow-up.

events.name: lsp.client.diagnostics
events.description: Observe LSP diagnostics from the client.
events.guidance: Useful for editor-driven validation or error aggregation.

events.name: lsp.updated
events.guidance: Keep this lightweight unless the repo depends heavily on LSP status.

events.name: message.part.removed
events.guidance: Rarely the first scaffold choice; use only for message lifecycle workflows.

events.name: message.part.updated
events.guidance: Use when the plugin needs fine-grained message stream awareness.

events.name: message.removed
events.guidance: Keep ownership clear if you also subscribe to catch-all event hooks.

events.name: message.updated
events.guidance: Use for logging or synchronization, not heavy computation.

events.name: permission.asked
events.description: React when OpenCode asks for permission.
events.guidance: Use this for notifications or state tracking, not as the primary guardrail.

events.name: permission.replied
events.guidance: Useful for analytics or workflow bookkeeping.

events.name: server.connected
events.description: React when a server connection is established.
events.guidance: Good for startup notifications or environment checks.

events.name: session.created
events.description: React when a session is created.
events.guidance: Use for bootstrap context or welcome-state logic.

events.name: session.compacted
events.guidance: Pair carefully with experimental compaction hooks to avoid duplicate logic.

events.name: session.deleted
events.description: React when a session is deleted.
events.guidance: Good for cleanup or archival bookkeeping.

events.name: session.diff
events.guidance: Use when the workflow depends on diff-level tracking.

events.name: session.error
events.guidance: Good for notifications, logging, or recovery prompts.

events.name: session.idle
events.description: React when a session goes idle.
events.guidance: The key post-turn validation trigger; usually coordinated through the catch-all event handler.

events.name: session.status
events.guidance: Use for progress reporting or state transitions.

events.name: session.updated
events.guidance: Useful for session metadata sync or instrumentation.

events.name: todo.updated
events.guidance: Use for todo-aware workflow nudges or analytics.

events.name: shell.env
events.description: Inject environment variables into shell execution.
events.guidance: Mutate output.env to add variables for shell commands.

events.name: tool.execute.after
events.description: Observe tool execution after it completes.
events.guidance: Use this to track edits, stash output, or trigger post-turn checks after the fact.

events.name: tool.execute.before
events.description: Inspect or rewrite a tool call before it runs.
events.guidance: Use this for guardrails, rewrites, or policy enforcement.

events.name: tui.prompt.append
events.description: React when prompt text is appended in the TUI.
events.guidance: Use when the TUI prompt needs augmentation or observation.

events.name: tui.command.execute
events.description: React when a TUI command executes.
events.guidance: Useful for lightweight command analytics or assistance.

events.name: tui.toast.show
events.description: React when a TUI toast is shown.
events.guidance: Best for UX instrumentation or notification coordination.

events.name: experimental.session.compacting
events.description: Inject or replace compaction context before OpenCode summarizes a session.
events.guidance: Use for context carry-over only. Do not build critical guardrails on this.
